evals.prompt: I'm working on the flask-apcore demo project at /Users/tercelyi/Workspace/aipartnerup/flask-apcore/examples/demo/. I want to know how good the module metadata quality is. Please assess all the .binding.yaml files in the apcore_modules/ directory and give me a detailed quality report with per-module scores.
evals.expected_output: A table showing 5 modules with per-dimension scores totaling 0-100. list_tasks.get should score ~65 (has readonly+cacheable annotations, no params), create_task.post ~25 (all defaults, no schema descriptions), get_task.get ~40, update_task.put ~40, delete_task.delete ~40. Average around 42.

evals.prompt: The metadata for the flask-apcore demo at /Users/tercelyi/Workspace/aipartnerup/flask-apcore/examples/demo/ is pretty bare-bones. Can you improve the create_task.post and delete_task.delete modules? Read the source code in app.py to understand what these functions actually do, then generate better descriptions, parameter descriptions, documentation, examples, tags, and the x-when-to-use metadata. Show me the changes before applying anything.
evals.expected_output: A diff showing proposed improvements for create_task.post and delete_task.delete. Should include: improved description (not placeholder), input_schema property descriptions for each parameter, documentation >50 chars, realistic examples with plausible task data, tags like ['tasks','crud'], and x-when-to-use metadata. Must NOT rename any parameters. Must show diff before writing.

evals.prompt: I need to check if the flask-apcore demo project at /Users/tercelyi/Workspace/aipartnerup/flask-apcore/examples/demo/ meets a metadata quality threshold of 60/100. Give me a pass/fail result.
evals.expected_output: Should report FAILED because the average score (~42) is below 60. Should list which modules are dragging the score down and suggest running refine.
